spec.name: Ruined Arch
spec.description: The first surviving structure of the ruins is a stone arch, its keystone still in place despite centuries of unassisted standing. It is clearly the entrance to something — a settlement, a keep — and the walls on either side, though reduced to scattered stones for most of their length, are still standing at the arch itself to a height of perhaps twelve feet. The carved figures in the arch's stonework are of a style completely different from the barrow carvings — these are figurative, depicting men and women in formal poses, their faces worn to smooth ovals by weather.
